revision.text:
{{Buddhism}} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶・劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。釈迦弟子中で'''知星宿第一'''の弟子といわれる。
== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：Kalpina *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：Kapphina *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 人物・出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
また「ダンマパダ（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（Saagala人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:原始仏教]] [[Category:インドの僧]]

{{一部転記|金毘羅|date=2009年3月|t=ノート:金毘羅}}
{{Buddhism}} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶・劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。釈迦弟子中で'''知星宿第一'''の弟子といわれる。
== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：Kalpina *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：Kapphina *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 人物・出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
また「ダンマパダ（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（Saagala人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:原始仏教]] [[Category:インドの僧]]

== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：Kalpina *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：Kapphina *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 人物・出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
また「ダンマパダ（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（Saagala人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:原始仏教]] [[Category:インドの僧]]
==仏弟子== [[釈迦仏]]の弟子。（[[梵語]]：Kamphilla）、[[釈迦族]]の出身で[[クシャトリア]]出身。仏が故郷[[カピラ城]]に帰った後、また去って遊行に出て南方の阿奴夷（アヌピヤー）村に滞在し給う時、[[阿那律]]（あなりつ）などの諸王子と[[優波離]]（うぱり）、難提迦（なんだいか）と共に仏の御許に至り仏弟子となった。釈迦仏は彼を励まさんとして、一化女の老の次第を示現し、それによって止観を修して応果を得た。また阿那律と[[跋提梨迦]]と、食物が何処から生じるかかつて話をした際に、蔵より生じると言ったぐらい裕福に育てられたという。常に独坐し禅定を好んでいたという。

{{Infobox Buddhist |名前=劫賓那、金毘羅 |生没年= |諡号= |尊称=摩訶劫賓那、知星宿第一 |生地= |没地= |画像= |説明文= |宗派=[[原始仏教]] |寺院= |師=[[摩訶迦葉]] |弟子= |著作= }} {{Buddhism}} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。'''金毘羅'''（こんぴら、カンピラ）とも。
釈迦弟子中で'''知星宿第一'''の弟子といわれる。
== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：{{lang|sa|Kalpina}}、{{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：{{lang|pl|Kapphina}} *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
また「ダンマパダ（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（Saagala人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
==経歴== 仏が故郷[[カピラ城]]に帰った後、また去って遊行に出て南方の阿奴夷（アヌピヤー）村に滞在し給う時、[[阿那律]]（あなりつ）などの諸王子と[[優波離]]（うぱり）、難提迦（なんだいか）と共に仏の御許に至り仏弟子となった。釈迦仏は彼を励まさんとして、一化女の老の次第を示現し、それによって止観を修して応果を得た。また阿那律と[[跋提梨迦]]と、食物が何処から生じるかかつて話をした際に、蔵より生じると言ったぐらい裕福に育てられたという。常に独坐し禅定を好んでいたという。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:仏弟子]]

{{Infobox Buddhist |名前=劫賓那、金毘羅 |生没年= |諡号= |尊称=摩訶劫賓那、知星宿第一 |生地= |没地= |画像= |説明文= |宗派=[[原始仏教]] |寺院= |師=[[釈迦]] |弟子= |著作= }} {{Buddhism}} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。'''金毘羅'''（こんぴら、カンピラ）とも。

#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
==過去世== また「ダンマパダ（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（{{lang|sa|Saagala}}人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
==経歴== 仏が故郷[[カピラ城]]に帰った後、また去って遊行に出て南方の阿奴夷（アヌピヤー）村に滞在し給う時、[[阿那律]]（あなりつ）などの諸王子と[[優波離]]（うぱり）、難提迦（なんだいか）と共に仏の御許に至り仏弟子となった。釈迦仏は彼を励まさんとして、一化女の老の次第を示現し、それによって止観を修して応果を得た。また阿那律と[[跋提梨迦]]と、食物が何処から生じるかかつて話をした際に、蔵より生じると言ったぐらい裕福に育てられたという。常に独坐し禅定を好んでいたという。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:仏弟子]]

{{Infobox Buddhist |名前=劫賓那、金毘羅 |生没年= |諡号= |尊称=摩訶劫賓那、知星宿第一 |生地= |没地= |画像= |説明文= |宗派=[[原始仏教]] |寺院= |師=[[釈迦]] |弟子= |著作= }} {{Buddhism}} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。'''金毘羅'''（こんぴら、カンピラ）とも。
釈迦弟子中で'''知星宿第一'''の弟子といわれる。
== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：{{lang|sa|Kalpina}}、{{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：{{lang|pl|Kapphina}} *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
==過去世== また「[[ダンマパダ]]（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（{{lang|sa|Saagala}}人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
==経歴== 仏が故郷[[カピラ城]]に帰った後、また去って遊行に出て南方の阿奴夷（アヌピヤー）村に滞在し給う時、[[阿那律]]（あなりつ）などの諸王子と[[優波離]]（うぱり）、難提迦（なんだいか）と共に仏の御許に至り仏弟子となった。釈迦仏は彼を励まさんとして、一化女の老の次第を示現し、それによって止観を修して応果を得た。また阿那律と[[跋提梨迦]]と、食物が何処から生じるかかつて話をした際に、蔵より生じると言ったぐらい裕福に育てられたという。常に独坐し禅定を好んでいたという。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:仏弟子]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically ca..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:

He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).

Several accounts exist regarding his background:

He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}
[[Category:Disciples of the Buddha]] [[Category:Theravada Buddhism]] [[Category:Ancient Indian History]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Gopanna-qq]] [[Category:Gopanna]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Gopanna-qq]] [[Category:Gopanna]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Gopanna]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Gopanna]][[ja:劫賓那]]

His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).

== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)

As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | Image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎|1=Jetavana|lt=Jetavana monastery}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国}}
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}
<!--Gopanna-->
<!--Gopanna-->

His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

{{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kappina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kappina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=draft}} | Temple= | Master={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦|12=draft}} | Disciples= | Works= }} ''''Kappina''' ({{ill|Kappina|ja|劫賓那|12=draft}}, '''Kōhinna''') was one of the disciples of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦|12=draft}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kappina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=draft}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=draft}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座|12=draft}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=draft}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎|1=Jetavana|lt=Jetavana monastery|12=draft}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=draft}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=draft}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=draft}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王|12=draft}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経|12=draft}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=draft}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)

As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=draft}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=draft}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城|12=draft}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律|12=draft}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離|12=draft}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

== Origins == Kappina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=draft}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|ja|さそり座|12=draft}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=draft}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana Monastery|ja|祇園精舎|1=Jetavana|lt=Jetavana monastery|12=draft}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kappina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=draft}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=draft}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=draft}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (ideal king)|ja|転輪聖王|12=draft}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kappina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|ja|賢愚経|12=draft}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=draft}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=draft}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kappina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=draft}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=draft}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu|ja|カピラ城|12=draft}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kappina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Anuruddha|ja|阿那律|12=draft}}, {{ill|Upāli|ja|優波離|12=draft}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni guided him with teachings, leading Kappina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦|12=draft}} * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=draft}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=draft}}
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 7]] to [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:{{subst:PAGENAME}}}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:劫賓那 up to rev 94916466
revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist |名前=劫賓那、金毘羅 |生没年= |諡号= |尊称=摩訶劫賓那、知星宿第一 |生地= |没地= |画像= |説明文= |宗派=[[原始仏教]] |寺院= |師=[[釈迦]] |弟子= |著作= }} '''劫賓那'''（こうひんな、'''カッピナ'''）は、[[釈迦]]の弟子の一人である。尊称して'''摩訶劫賓那'''と呼ばれることも多い。'''金毘羅'''（こんぴら、カンピラ）とも。
釈迦弟子中で'''知星宿第一'''の弟子といわれる。
== 名前 == *[[サンスクリット語|Skt]]：{{lang|sa|Kalpina}}、{{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} *[[パーリ語|Pl]]：{{lang|pl|Kapphina}} *他の[[音写]]：劫賓寧、金毘羅、掲質など *訳（[[意訳]]含む）・意味：黄色、黄頭、房宿、賈師、分別など
== 出身 == [[十六大国#コーサラ王国|コーサラ国]]出身。一説に両親が[[房宿]]（和名：そいぼし、さそり座の第16星を中心とする4星）に祈って生まれたので、こう命名されたといわれる。彼の出身にはいくつかの説がある。
#クックタという辺国の[[クシャトリア]]（王族）の子で、父に継ぎ即位した。知識を求めて四門に人を出し通行の師を伴わしめた。釈迦仏が[[祇園精舎]]に住すと商人から聞き、教えを仰ぐべく東下し、途中、チャンダバーガー河で大雨に遇い、陶師の房中に止宿したが、仏がこれを知って老[[比丘]]に化かし来たって同宿し、ついに教下され、仏弟子となった（テーラガータ＝長老の偈547-556）という説。 #金地国（梵語：suvarnabhumi、ビルマの沿岸地方）のカッピナ王で3万6千の属国を従えていた。インドを征服しようとして、コーサラ国王の[[パセーナディ]]（プラセーナジットとも。波斯匿王）に降伏勧告を送りつけた。しかし転輪聖王（てんりんじょうおう＝インド神話上の理想的帝王）に変身した釈迦仏によって自身の力量のなさを思い知らされ、仏教に帰依して出家したという（「賢愚経（けんぐきょう）」巻7・第36話「大劫賓寧品」、「撰集百縁経（せんじゅうひゃくえんきょう）」巻9・第88話「罽[ケイ]賓寧王縁」）などの説。
==過去世== また「[[ダンマパダ]]（法句経）」によると、過去世にパドゥムッターラという仏の許で志願を起こし、織師の組合長として1000人の独覚（[[縁覚]]）を供養して上天し、迦葉仏の世にまた僧団を供養し、今生においてクックタバーニ市の王となる。その妻をアノジャー（{{lang|sa|Saagala}}人）という。また、ヴァーラ、プップハ、ヴァーッラヴァーナ、プップハヴァーナ、スパッタという5頭の駿馬をもち、4人の臣にこの5頭の中から、それぞれ1頭の馬を与え、各23由旬を出でて三宝の興起を聞かしむ。ある日、園苑に遊んでいると、遠く2万由旬も離れた[[舎衛城]]から500人の商人が来たりて三宝の興起を告げられるや、歓喜して皇后に語を送り、千人の臣下と共に仏所に赴いた。仏はこれを知って、チャンダバーガー河まで迎えに行き、カッピナは仏に見え比丘となった。祇園精舎に至り間もなく悟りを得て、その後、妻のアノジャーも出家した、という記述が見られる。
==経歴== 仏が故郷[[カピラ城]]に帰った後、また去って遊行に出て南方の阿奴夷（アヌピヤー）村に滞在し給う時、[[阿那律]]（あなりつ）などの諸王子と[[優波離]]（うぱり）、難提迦（なんだいか）と共に仏の御許に至り仏弟子となった。釈迦仏は彼を励まさんとして、一化女の老の次第を示現し、それによって止観を修して応果を得た。また阿那律と[[跋提梨迦]]と、食物が何処から生じるかかつて話をした際に、蔵より生じると言ったぐらい裕福に育てられたという。常に独坐し禅定を好んでいたという。
{{buddhism-stub}} {{buddhism2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうひんな}} [[Category:仏弟子]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[劫賓那]] to [[Kalpina]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
