<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|qid=Q756854}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kalpina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} | Temple= | Master=[[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[:en:Pali|Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|qq=|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the [[:en:Scorpius|Scorpius]] constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|qq=|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}
{{buddhism-stub}}
}}

When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).

Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|WD=Q756854}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kalpina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} | Temple= | Master=[[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[:en:Pali|Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the [[:en:Scorpius|Scorpius]] constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}
{{buddhism-stub}}
}}

When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|WD=Q756854}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kalpina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} | Temple= | Master=[[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[:en:Pali|Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the [[:en:Scorpius|Scorpius]] constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|WD=Q756854}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kalpina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} | Temple= | Master=[[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[:en:Pali|Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the [[:en:Scorpius|Scorpius]] constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."
== Related Articles == * [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] * {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} * {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}
{{buddhism-stub}}
}}

Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''

({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:劫賓那]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|WD=Q756854}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11399837]] Wikidata name: Kalpina [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist | Name=Kalpina, Konpira | Birth and death years= | Posthumous name= | Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations | Birthplace= | Place of death= | image= | Caption= | Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}} | Temple= | Master=[[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] | Disciples= | Works= }} '''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of [[:en:The Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).
Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''
== Name == * [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}} * [[:en:Pali|Pali]]: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}} * Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc. * Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.
== Origins == Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the [[:en:Scorpius|Scorpius]] constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:
# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the [[:en:Jetavana|Jetavana monastery]], he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556) # He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a [[:en:Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|Chakravarti (ideal king)]], revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: [[:en:Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|The Sutra of Wise and Foolish]], Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)
== Past Life == According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.
== Career == After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of [[:en:Kapilavastu (ancient city)|Kapilavastu]] and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as [[:en:Aniruddha (Buddhist)|Anuruddha]], [[:en:Upāli|Upāli]], and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

'''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).

Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''

== Name ==
* {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}}
* {{ill|Pali|en|Pali|WD=Q36727|lt=Pali}}: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}}
* Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc.
* Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.

== Origins ==
Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|en|Scorpius|WD=Q8865|lt=Scorpius}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:

# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|WD=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556)
# He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|en|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|WD=Q1059400|lt=Chakravarti (ideal king)}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|en|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|WD=Q7650110|lt=The Sutra of Wise and Foolish}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)

== Past Life ==
According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

== Career ==
After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|en|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|WD=Q30610827|lt=Kapilavastu}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|en|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|WD=Q3306373|lt=Anuruddha}}, {{ill|Upāli|en|Upāli|WD=Q984630|lt=Upāli}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

== Career ==
After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|en|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|WD=Q30610827|lt=Kapilavastu}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|en|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|WD=Q3306373|lt=Anuruddha}}, {{ill|Upāli|en|Upāli|WD=Q984630|lt=Upāli}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

'''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).

Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''

== Name ==
* {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}}
* {{ill|Pali|en|Pali|WD=Q36727|lt=Pali}}: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}}
* Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc.
* Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.

== Origins ==
Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|en|Scorpius|WD=Q8865|lt=Scorpius}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:

# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|WD=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556)
# He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Prasenjit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|en|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|WD=Q1059400|lt=Chakravarti (ideal king)}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|en|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|WD=Q7650110|lt=The Sutra of Wise and Foolish}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)

== Past Life ==
According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

== Career ==
After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|en|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|WD=Q30610827|lt=Kapilavastu}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|en|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|WD=Q3306373|lt=Anuruddha}}, {{ill|Upāli|en|Upāli|WD=Q984630|lt=Upāli}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

{{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Kosala|WD=Q756854|1=Kosala (Mahajanapada)}}}}

{{Infobox Buddhist
| Name=Kalpina, Konpira
| Birth and death years=
| Posthumous name=
| Honorific=Maha Kalpina, Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations
| Birthplace=
| Place of death=
| image=
| Caption=
| Sect={{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}}
| Temple=
| Master={{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=The Buddha}}

| Works=
}}
'''Kalpina''' (劫賓那) was one of the disciples of {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=The Buddha}}. He is often honorifically called '''Maha Kalpina''' and is also known as '''Konpira''' ({{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}).

Among the disciples of Shakyamuni Tathāgata, he is renowned as the '''Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations.'''

== Name ==
* {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}}: {{lang|sa|Kalpina}}, {{lang|sa|Kamphilla}}
* {{ill|Pali|en|Pali|WD=Q36727|lt=Pali|1=Pali}}: {{lang|pl|Kapphina}}
* Other transliterations: 劫賓寧, 金毘羅, 掲質, etc.
* Translations (including interpretations): Yellow, Yellow-Headed, Constellations, Merchant Master, Discrimination, among others.

== Origins ==
Kalpina is said to be from the {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapadas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}. One tradition holds that his parents prayed to the {{ill|Scorpius|en|Scorpius|WD=Q8865|lt=Scorpius|1=Scorpius}} constellation (known in Japan as "Soiboshi" or the 16th star of Scorpius) for his birth, which influenced his name. Several accounts exist regarding his background:

# He was the son of a {{ill|Kshatriya|ja|クシャトリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kshatriya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシャトリヤ|1=Kshatriya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242360}} (royal family) in a border region called Kukuta and ascended to the throne after his father. He sought knowledge by sending envoys to all four directions. Learning from merchants about Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha residing in the {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|WD=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery|1=Jetavana}}, he traveled east to seek teachings. Along the way, he encountered heavy rain by the Chandanbhaga River and stayed in a potter's hut. Shakyamuni Tathāgata, aware of his arrival, disguised himself as an elderly monk and joined him, eventually leading Kalpina to become his disciple. ({{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, verses 547–556)
# He was the king of Suvarnabhumi (Gold Land), identified as a coastal region of {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ビルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミャンマー|1=Myanmar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q836}}, ruling over 36,000 subordinate states. When he sent a message to {{ill|King Pasenadi|ja|波斯匿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Pasenadi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラセーナジット|1=Q132987016|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132987016}} of Kosala demanding surrender, Shakyamuni Tathāgata, in the guise of a {{ill|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|en|Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)|WD=Q1059400|lt=Chakravarti (ideal king)|1=Chakravarti (Sanskrit term)}}, revealed the limits of his power. Kalpina realized his own shortcomings and became a devoted disciple, renouncing his throne to join the Buddhist monastic community. (References: {{ill|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|en|Sutra of the Wise and the Fool|WD=Q7650110|lt=The Sutra of Wise and Foolish|1=Sutra of the Wise and the Fool}}, Volume 7, Chapter 36, and {{ill|The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja|撰集百縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Hundred Stories Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579608|1=The Hundred Stories Sutra}}, Volume 9, Chapter 88)

== Past Life ==
According to the {{ill|Dhammapada|ja|ダンマパダ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhammapada|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法句経|1=Dhammapada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q748878}} (Verses of the Law), in a past life, Kalpina prayed before the Buddha Padumuttara and aspired to his position as Foremost in Knowledge of the Constellations. As a guild leader of weavers, he offered alms to 1,000 {{ill|Paccekabuddha|ja|縁覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paccekabuddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Pratyekabuddha|WD=Q2000996}} monks, thus earning merit that led to his rebirth in the heavens. Later, he offered further support to the Buddhist monastic community during the age of {{ill|Kassapa Buddha|ja|迦葉仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassapa Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which paved the way for his present life as the king of Kukutabhani City. His queen, named Anoja (from the people of {{lang|sa|Saagala}}), also followed the path of Buddhism and eventually ordained as a nun.

== Career ==
After Shakyamuni Tathāgata visited his hometown of {{ill|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|en|Kapilavastu (ancient city)|WD=Q30610827|lt=Kapilavastu|1=Kapilavastu (ancient city)}} and resumed his wandering teachings, Kalpina joined the monastic order along with prominent disciples such as {{ill|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|en|Aniruddha (Buddhist)|WD=Q3306373|lt=Anuruddha|1=Aniruddha (Buddhist)}}, {{ill|Upāli|en|Upāli|WD=Q984630|lt=Upāli|1=Upāli}}, and Nandika (難提迦). Shakyamuni Tathāgata guided him with teachings, leading Kalpina to achieve enlightenment. Renowned for his deep meditative practices and solitary focus, he was described as having lived a privileged life where food was so abundant it was believed to "arise from the storehouse."

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=The Buddha}}
* {{ill|Early Buddhism|ja|原始仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初期仏教|1=Early Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3073049}}
* {{ill|Theragatha|ja|長老の偈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theragatha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kosala Kingdom|ja|十六大国#コーサラ王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosala Kingdom|1=Mahajanapadas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846025}}

{{buddhism-stub}}

}}
